analyses.title: Effect of goal-directed fluid resuscitation therapy based on peripheral perfusion index on acute skin failure in elderly critically ill patients: A randomized controlled trial.
analyses.oneLineSummary: In elderly critically ill patients, adding a peripheral perfusion index (PPI ≥1.4) target to fluid resuscitation roughly halved 7-day acute skin failure without a mortality change.
analyses.oneLineSummary_ko: 고령 중증 환자에서 말초관류지수(PPI ≥1.4) 목표를 수액소생술에 추가하자 7일 급성 피부부전이 약 절반으로 줄었으나 사망률 변화는 없었다.
analyses.whyItMatters: Acute skin failure is common and morbid in elderly ICU patients, and this trial suggests a cheap, noninvasive bedside PPI target may reduce it while improving lactate clearance and limiting cumulative positive fluid balance—an appealing add-on to standard macrohemodynamic resuscitation goals.
analyses.whyItMatters_ko: 급성 피부부전은 고령 중환자에서 흔하고 이환율이 높은데, 이 연구는 저렴하고 비침습적인 침상 PPI 목표가 젖산 청소율을 개선하고 누적 양성 수액균형을 줄이면서 이를 감소시킬 수 있음을 시사하여 기존 대혈역학 소생 목표에 매력적인 보조 지표가 될 수 있다.
analyses.clinicalQuestion: In elderly critically ill patients requiring early fluid resuscitation, does adding a peripheral perfusion index (PPI ≥1.4) target to conventional macrohemodynamic goals reduce the 7-day incidence of acute skin failure?
analyses.clinicalQuestion_ko: 조기 수액소생술이 필요한 고령 중증 환자에서 기존 대혈역학 목표에 말초관류지수(PPI ≥1.4) 목표를 추가하면 7일 급성 피부부전 발생률이 감소하는가?

studyDetails.design: Single-center prospective, randomized, parallel-controlled trial analyzed by intention-to-treat.
studyDetails.design_ko: 단일기관 전향적, 무작위, 평행 대조 시험으로 치료의도(ITT) 분석.
studyDetails.setting: Intensive care unit setting; author affiliations indicate Hai'an People's Hospital and affiliated centers in Nantong, Jiangsu Province, China (per PubMed metadata). Enrollment January–December 2025.
studyDetails.setting_ko: 중환자실 환경; 저자 소속은 중국 장쑤성 난퉁의 하이안 인민병원 및 관련 기관(PubMed 메타데이터). 등록 기간 2025년 1~12월.
studyDetails.sampleSize: 216 elderly critically ill patients enrolled; 204 analyzed in the ITT population (103 intervention, 101 control). Reason for the 12-patient reduction is not available in the accessible sources.
studyDetails.sampleSize_ko: 고령 중증 환자 216명 등록; ITT 집단에서 204명 분석(중재군 103명, 대조군 101명). 12명 감소 사유는 접근 가능한 자료에 없음.
studyDetails.eligibility: Elderly critically ill patients requiring early active fluid resuscitation (MeSH: Aged, Aged 80 and over, Critical Illness). Exact age cutoff, severity scores, and exclusion criteria are not available in the accessible sources.
studyDetails.eligibility_ko: 조기 적극적 수액소생술이 필요한 고령 중증 환자(MeSH: 노인, 80세 이상, 중증질환). 정확한 연령 기준, 중증도 점수, 제외 기준은 접근 가능한 자료에 없음.
studyDetails.interventionDetails: PPI-guided goal-directed resuscitation using PPI ≥1.4 as an additional resuscitation endpoint on top of conventional macrohemodynamic goals. Specific fluid types, doses, routes, and total volumes and the PPI measurement device/protocol are not available in the accessible sources.
studyDetails.interventionDetails_ko: 기존 대혈역학 목표에 더해 PPI ≥1.4를 추가 소생 종료점으로 사용하는 PPI 유도 목표지향 소생술. 구체적 수액 종류·용량·경로·총량 및 PPI 측정 기기/프로토콜은 접근 가능한 자료에 없음.
studyDetails.comparatorDetails: Conventional resuscitation group managed with fluid therapy based on routine indicators (macrohemodynamic goals) without a PPI target. Specific protocol details not available in the accessible sources.
studyDetails.comparatorDetails_ko: PPI 목표 없이 통상 지표(대혈역학 목표)에 기반한 수액요법으로 관리된 통상 소생군. 구체적 프로토콜은 접근 가능한 자료에 없음.
studyDetails.followUp: Primary outcome assessed within 7 days of ICU admission; secondary outcomes included 72-hour fluid balance and 28-day all-cause mortality.
studyDetails.followUp_ko: 1차 결과는 중환자실 입실 후 7일 이내 평가; 2차 결과는 72시간 수액균형과 28일 전체 사망률 포함.
studyDetails.sourceBasis: Abstract (Medicine, 2026), PubMed metadata (affiliations, MeSH), and Crossref metadata (funders, publication data). No PMCID/open full text available.
studyDetails.sourceBasis_ko: 초록(Medicine, 2026), PubMed 메타데이터(소속, MeSH), Crossref 메타데이터(연구비, 출판 정보). PMCID/공개 전문 없음.

pico.population: Elderly critically ill patients requiring early active fluid resuscitation (204 analyzed).
pico.intervention: PPI-guided goal-directed fluid resuscitation using PPI ≥1.4 as an additional endpoint plus conventional macrohemodynamic goals.
pico.comparison: Conventional fluid resuscitation guided by routine macrohemodynamic indicators only.
pico.outcome: 7-day incidence of acute skin failure; secondary: time to ASF onset, 6-hour lactate clearance, 72-hour fluid balance, 28-day mortality.

pico_ko.population: 조기 적극적 수액소생술이 필요한 고령 중증 환자(204명 분석).
pico_ko.intervention: PPI ≥1.4를 추가 종료점으로 하는 PPI 유도 목표지향 수액소생술 + 기존 대혈역학 목표.
pico_ko.comparison: 통상 대혈역학 지표만으로 유도된 통상 수액소생술.
pico_ko.outcome: 7일 급성 피부부전 발생률; 2차: ASF 발생까지 시간, 6시간 젖산 청소율, 72시간 수액균형, 28일 사망률.

detailedPico.population: 216 elderly critically ill patients requiring early active fluid resuscitation were enrolled and 204 analyzed by ITT (103 intervention, 101 control). MeSH indicates both sexes and patients aged 65+ including 80+.
detailedPico.population_ko: 조기 적극적 수액소생술이 필요한 고령 중증 환자 216명이 등록되어 204명이 ITT로 분석됨(중재군 103명, 대조군 101명). MeSH상 남녀 모두, 65세 이상(80세 이상 포함).
detailedPico.countrySetting: ICUs in China; author affiliations point to Hai'an People's Hospital and affiliated centers, Nantong, Jiangsu Province (PubMed metadata). Enrollment Jan–Dec 2025.
detailedPico.countrySetting_ko: 중국의 중환자실; 저자 소속은 장쑤성 난퉁의 하이안 인민병원 및 관련 기관(PubMed 메타데이터). 등록 2025년 1~12월.
detailedPico.intervention: Goal-directed resuscitation adding PPI ≥1.4 as a supplementary target to conventional macrohemodynamic endpoints. Fluid type/dose/route not available in accessible sources.
detailedPico.intervention_ko: 기존 대혈역학 종료점에 PPI ≥1.4를 보조 목표로 추가한 목표지향 소생술. 수액 종류/용량/경로는 접근 가능한 자료에 없음.
detailedPico.comparator: Conventional resuscitation using routine indicators (macrohemodynamic goals) without a PPI target.
detailedPico.comparator_ko: PPI 목표 없이 통상 지표(대혈역학 목표)를 사용한 통상 소생술.

outcomes.label: Primary
outcomes.statistics: P = .009; absolute difference ~15.1 percentage points (RR/OR and CI not reported)
outcomes.interpretation: PPI-guided resuscitation cut the 7-day ASF rate roughly in half, a statistically significant reduction.
outcomes.interpretation_ko: PPI 유도 소생술은 7일 ASF 발생률을 약 절반으로 줄였으며 통계적으로 유의했다.
outcomes.statPrimer: P<0.05 is conventionally significant; here P=.009 favors the intervention. An absolute risk difference of ~15% corresponds to roughly 1 fewer ASF case per ~7 patients treated (NNT), though the authors did not report a formal NNT or CI.
outcomes.statPrimer_ko: P<0.05는 통상적으로 유의함을 의미하며, 여기서 P=.009로 중재군에 유리하다. 절대위험차 약 15%는 대략 7명 치료당 1명의 ASF 감소(NNT)에 해당하나 저자가 공식 NNT나 CI는 제시하지 않았다.

outcomes.label: Secondary
outcomes.result: 5.2 days intervention vs 3.1 days control, log-rank P = .012
outcomes.interpretation: Among patients who developed ASF, onset was delayed by about 2 days with PPI guidance.
outcomes.interpretation_ko: ASF가 발생한 환자에서 PPI 유도 시 발생이 약 2일 지연되었다.
outcomes.statPrimer: The log-rank test compares time-to-event curves; P<0.05 indicates the timing distributions differ significantly, here favoring later onset with the intervention.
outcomes.statPrimer_ko: 로그순위 검정은 사건 발생 시간 곡선을 비교하며, P<0.05는 시간 분포가 유의하게 다름을 의미하고 여기서는 중재군의 늦은 발생에 유리하다.

outcomes.label: Secondary
outcomes.interpretation: PPI-guided patients cleared lactate more effectively in the first 6 hours, suggesting better early perfusion.
outcomes.interpretation_ko: PPI 유도 환자는 초기 6시간 동안 젖산을 더 효과적으로 청소하여 조기 관류가 더 나았음을 시사한다.
outcomes.statPrimer: Means ± SD compared between groups; P<0.05 indicates the difference is statistically significant. A higher clearance percentage reflects greater lactate reduction.
outcomes.statPrimer_ko: 군 간 평균±표준편차 비교; P<0.05는 통계적으로 유의함을 의미한다. 청소율이 높을수록 젖산 감소가 큼을 나타낸다.

outcomes.label: Secondary
outcomes.outcome: Cumulative positive fluid balance at 72 hours
outcomes.result: +1250 ± 980 mL intervention vs +1850 ± 1100 mL control, P < .001
outcomes.interpretation: PPI guidance was associated with ~600 mL less fluid accumulation, indicating less fluid overload.
outcomes.interpretation_ko: PPI 유도는 약 600 mL 적은 수액 축적과 연관되어 수액 과부하가 덜함을 나타낸다.
outcomes.statPrimer: Mean difference between groups; P<0.05 indicates statistical significance. A lower positive balance means less net fluid retained.
outcomes.statPrimer_ko: 군 간 평균 차이; P<0.05는 통계적 유의성을 의미한다. 양성 균형이 낮을수록 순 저류 수액이 적음을 뜻한다.

outcomes.label: Secondary
outcomes.result: No significant difference between groups (exact values not reported)
outcomes.result_ko: 두 군 간 유의한 차이 없음(정확한 수치 미보고)
outcomes.statistics: Reported as nonsignificant; effect estimate and CI not provided
outcomes.interpretation: The trial did not show a survival benefit, though it was likely underpowered for mortality.
outcomes.interpretation_ko: 이 시험은 생존 이득을 보이지 못했으나 사망률에 대해서는 검정력이 부족했을 가능성이 높다.
outcomes.statPrimer: 'Nonsignificant' means P≥0.05, so no statistically significant difference was established; this does not prove equivalence, especially with a small sample.
outcomes.statPrimer_ko: '유의하지 않음'은 P≥0.05를 의미하여 통계적으로 유의한 차이가 확립되지 않았음을 뜻하며, 특히 표본이 작을 때 동등함을 증명하지는 않는다.

keyFindings: 7-day acute skin failure incidence was significantly lower with PPI-guided resuscitation: 14.6% (15/103) vs 29.7% (30/101), P = .009.

PPI guidance improved resuscitation quality: higher 6-hour lactate clearance (28.5% ± 12.3% vs 21.8% ± 14.1%, P = .001) and lower 72-hour cumulative positive fluid balance (+1250 ± 980 mL vs +1850 ± 1100 mL, P < .001); time to ASF onset was prolonged (5.2 vs 3.1 days, log-rank P = .012).
28-day all-cause mortality did not differ significantly between groups.
keyFindings_ko: 7일 급성 피부부전 발생률은 PPI 유도 소생술에서 유의하게 낮았다: 14.6%(15/103) vs 29.7%(30/101), P = .009.
PPI 유도는 소생 질을 개선했다: 6시간 젖산 청소율 상승(28.5% ± 12.3% vs 21.8% ± 14.1%, P = .001), 72시간 누적 양성 수액균형 감소(+1250 ± 980 mL vs +1850 ± 1100 mL, P < .001); ASF 발생까지 시간 연장(5.2일 vs 3.1일, 로그순위 P = .012).
28일 전체 사망률은 두 군 간 유의한 차이가 없었다.
analyses.conclusion: A PPI-based goal-directed fluid resuscitation strategy significantly reduced the risk of acute skin failure in elderly critically ill patients, improved early perfusion markers (lactate clearance), and optimized fluid balance, without a demonstrated mortality benefit. This noninvasive bedside monitoring approach offers a new rationale for protecting skin health during resuscitation.
analyses.conclusion_ko: PPI 기반 목표지향 수액소생술은 고령 중증 환자에서 급성 피부부전 위험을 유의하게 낮추고, 조기 관류 지표(젖산 청소율)를 개선하며 수액균형을 최적화했으나 사망률 이득은 입증되지 않았다. 이 비침습적 침상 모니터링 접근법은 소생 중 피부 건강 보호에 대한 새로운 근거를 제공한다.
analyses.clinicalTakeaway: Consider adding a noninvasive PPI target (≥1.4) to conventional macrohemodynamic endpoints in elderly ICU patients undergoing fluid resuscitation to reduce acute skin failure and avoid fluid overload, recognizing this is a single-center trial with no mortality benefit and no accessible protocol details on PPI measurement or fluid dosing.
analyses.clinicalTakeaway_ko: 수액소생술을 받는 고령 중환자에서 급성 피부부전 감소와 수액 과부하 회피를 위해 기존 대혈역학 지표에 비침습적 PPI 목표(≥1.4)를 추가하는 것을 고려할 수 있으나, 이는 단일기관 연구로 사망률 이득이 없고 PPI 측정·수액 용량에 대한 상세 프로토콜을 확인할 수 없다는 점을 유의해야 한다.
analyses.limitations: Single-center trial from one Chinese hospital system limiting generalizability; enrolled 216 but analyzed 204 (attrition reason not available in accessible sources); no reported blinding (skin/perfusion assessment susceptible to bias); acute skin failure definition, PPI measurement device/protocol, and specific fluid types, doses, routes, and total volumes are not available in the accessible sources; the trial was not clearly registered (no ClinicalTrials.gov ID in the PubMed record); effect estimates (RR/OR, absolute risk difference, NNT, CIs) for the primary outcome were not reported beyond raw percentages and P values; likely underpowered for mortality.
analyses.limitations_ko: 단일 중국 병원 시스템의 단일기관 연구로 일반화가 제한됨; 216명 등록 중 204명 분석(탈락 사유는 접근 가능한 자료에 없음); 눈가림 여부 미보고(피부/관류 평가는 편향에 취약); 급성 피부부전 정의, PPI 측정 기기/프로토콜, 구체적 수액 종류·용량·경로·총량은 접근 가능한 자료에 없음; 임상시험 등록이 명확하지 않음(PubMed 기록에 ClinicalTrials.gov ID 없음); 1차 결과의 효과추정치(RR/OR, 절대위험차, NNT, CI)는 원자료 백분율과 P값 외에 보고되지 않음; 사망률에 대해서는 검정력 부족 가능성.
analyses.edIcuApplicability: Directly relevant to ED and ICU clinicians resuscitating elderly patients, where PPI is a readily available, noninvasive pulse-oximeter–derived measure. The strategy could be piloted as a supplementary perfusion endpoint alongside lactate and fluid-balance monitoring. However, absence of standardized ASF criteria, PPI protocol specifics, blinding, and multicenter validation means it should be viewed as hypothesis-generating rather than practice-changing until confirmed.
analyses.edIcuApplicability_ko: PPI는 맥박산소측정기에서 손쉽게 얻는 비침습적 지표이므로 고령 환자를 소생하는 응급실·중환자실 임상의에게 직접적으로 관련이 있다. 이 전략은 젖산 및 수액균형 모니터링과 함께 보조 관류 지표로 시범 적용해 볼 수 있다. 그러나 표준화된 ASF 기준, PPI 프로토콜 세부사항, 눈가림, 다기관 검증이 없으므로 확증 전까지는 진료를 바꾸기보다 가설 생성 연구로 보아야 한다.
analysisNotes: Analysis based on abstract plus PubMed and Crossref metadata; no PMCID/open full text available.
No ClinicalTrials.gov registration ID present in the PubMed record; trial registration status uncertain.
Primary outcome reported only as raw percentages and P values; RR/OR/NNT and CIs not provided in accessible sources.
Sample size: 216 enrolled, 204 analyzed (ITT: 103 intervention, 101 control); attrition reason not stated in accessible sources.
Country/setting (Nantong, Jiangsu, China) inferred from author affiliations in PubMed metadata, not the abstract body.

paper.title: Effect of goal-directed fluid resuscitation therapy based on peripheral perfusion index on acute skin failure in elderly critically ill patients: A randomized controlled trial.
paper.abstract:
BACKGROUND: This study aimed to evaluate the effect of the peripheral perfusion index (PPI)-based goal-directed fluid resuscitation strategy for reducing the incidence of acute skin failure (ASF) in elderly critically ill patients.
METHODS: We conducted a prospective, randomized, parallel-controlled study by enrolling 216 elderly critically ill patients who required early active fluid resuscitation from January 2025 to December 2025. They were randomly assigned to 2 categories: PPI-guided and conventional resuscitation groups. The intervention group considered PPI ≥1.4 as an additional resuscitation endpoint with conventional macrohemodynamic goals. Based on routine indicators, the control group received fluid management only. Our primary outcome comprised the incidence of ASF within 7 days of intensive care unit admission. Secondary outcomes included time to ASF onset, resuscitation quality indicators, organ function, and long-term prognosis. Data were analyzed according to the intention-to-treat principle.
RESULTS: Our study involved 204 patients (103 intervention and 101 control). The intervention group (14.6%, 15/103) experienced a significantly reduced 7-day incidence of ASF compared with the control group (29.7%, 30/101, P = .009). However, the median time to ASF onset was prolonged in the intervention group (5.2 days vs 3.1 days, log-rank P = .012). Regarding the resuscitation quality, the intervention group demonstrated a higher 6-hour lactate clearance rate (28.5% ± 12.3% vs 21.8% ± 14.1%, P = .001) and reduced cumulative positive fluid balance at 72 hours (+1250 ± 980 mL vs +1850 ± 1100 mL, P < .001). The 28-day all-cause mortality between the groups remained nonsignificant.
CONCLUSION: A PPI-based goal-directed fluid resuscitation strategy significantly reduces the risk of ASF in elderly critically ill patients, improves early perfusion marker assessments, and optimizes fluid management. This noninvasive, bedside monitoring approach provides a new clinical rationale for protecting skin health in critically ill patients.
authorAffiliations: Department of Dermatology, Hai'an People's Hospital, Nantong, Jiangsu Province, China.

Public bibliographic metadata from Crossref:

Crossref abstract/snippet: Background: This study aimed to evaluate the effect of the peripheral perfusion index (PPI)-based goal-directed fluid resuscitation strategy for reducing the incidence of acute skin failure (ASF) in elderly critically ill patients. Methods: We conducted a prospective, randomized, parallel-controlled study by enrolling 216 elderly critically ill patients who required early active fluid resuscitation from January 2025 to December 2025. They were randomly assigned to 2 categories: PPI-guided and conventional resuscitation groups. The intervention group considered PPI ≥1.4 as an additional resuscitation endpoint with conventional macrohemodynamic goals. Based on routine indicators, the control group received fluid management only. Our primary outcome comprised the incidence of ASF within 7 days of intensive care unit admission. Secondary outcomes included time to ASF onset, resuscitation quality indicators, organ function, and long-term prognosis. Data were analyzed according to the intention-to-treat principle. Results: Our study involved 204 patients (103 intervention and 101 control). The intervention group (14.6%, 15/103) experienced a significantly reduced 7-day incidence of ASF compared with the control group (29.7%, 30/101, P = .009). However, the median time to ASF onset was prolonged in the intervention group (5.2 days vs 3.1 days, log-rank P = .012). Regarding the resuscitation quality, the intervention group demonstrated a higher 6-hour lactate clearance rate (28.5% ± 12.3% vs 21.8% ± 14.1%, P = .001) and reduced cumulative positive fluid balance at 72 hours (+1250 ± 980 mL vs +1850 ± 1100 mL, P < .001). The 28-day all-cause mortality between the groups remained nonsignificant. Conclusion: A PPI-based goal-directed fluid resuscitation strategy significantly re

selected.rationale_ko: 성인 중증 환자를 대상으로 한 실제 결과가 있는 무작위대조시험(RCT)으로, 말초관류지수(PPI) 기반 목표지향 수액소생 전략이 급성 피부부전 발생을 유의하게 감소시켰다는 중환자실 임상에 직접 적용 가능한 개입 연구다. 후보 목록 대부분이 결과 없는 연구 프로토콜, MIMIC 기반 후향적 예측모델/노모그램, 신생아·소아 대상 연구, QI/질적연구인 데 비해 성인 EM/CCM 임상에 가장 실용적이고 실행 가능한 근거를 제공한다. 다만 단일기관, 표본수(N=204)가 작고 일차결과(급성 피부부전)가 다소 협소해 실무 변화 가능성은 제한적이므로 중간 정도 우선순위로 평가한다.

notes_ko: 후보군 전반이 임상적 실행가능성이 낮음: 다수가 연구 프로토콜(#4, #7, #21, #22), 결과 없는 설계 논문이거나 MIMIC-IV/eICU 기반 후향적 예측 바이오마커·노모그램 연구(#8, #15, #19, #25, #26)로 직접 진료 변화 근거가 약함.
높은 임팩트 저널 RCT인 #2(옥테니딘, JAMA Network Open)는 신생아 NICU 대상으로 성인 EM/CCM 뉴스레터 적합성이 낮아 제외.
#18(소생 후 병원전 기도교체)과 #20(미성숙 과립구 세균혈증 감별)은 EM 관련성이 있으나 관찰연구로 근거 수준이 낮음.
최종적으로 성인 중환자 대상 실제 결과가 있는 유일한 핵심 RCT인 #30을 Top 1으로 선정했으나, 단일기관·소규모·협소한 일차결과로 인해 임상 우선순위 점수는 보통 수준으로 부여함.
